title: dianthus pinifolius subsp. brevifolius
intro: Dianthus pinifolius subsp. brevifolius is a subspecies of the Dianthus pinifolius plant, commonly known as pine-pink or mountain pink, which is a type of flowering plant belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Dianthus pinifolius subsp. brevifolius include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe sore throats, and promote healthy digestion. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
